question.label: Wait too long
question.name: When you wait too long to ask, what are you usually waiting to know first?
question.description: Drills into his 'wait too long' pattern to surface the specific uncertainty he is trying to resolve alone.

answer: When feedback is specific and fast, I move much quicker.

assessment.note: He names a specific missing condition—fast, specific feedback—that currently limits his speed, which is a strong clarity gap; the fact that he moves quickly once he has it shows underlying engagement.

new_queue.label: Thread follow
new_queue.name: feedback specific fast, — can you say more about what that means for you right now?

new_queue.label: Handoff gaps
new_queue.name: Where do the handoffs still feel fuzzy?

new_queue.label: Shipping hesitation
new_queue.name: What feels uncertain right before you ship?

new_queue.label: Support cadence
new_queue.name: What kind of check-in from me would help you most over the next two weeks?

new_queue.label: Fastest unblock
new_queue.name: What would make the next two weeks feel steadier for you?

history.answer_excerpt: I can do the tasks, but I still second-guess whether my approach matches team expectations.
When feedback is specific and fast, I move much quicker.
I can do the tasks, but I still second-guess whether my approach matches team expectations.
Local setup is better now, but I still lose time when the design and API details do not match.
I am not always sure who should approve what before I push.
I usually wait too long before asking because I want to solve things myself first.
When feedback is specific and fast, I move much quicker.
